PRESIDENTI AL CONFIDANT AND UNOFFICIAL PRESS ADVISOR EUGEN1I0 SOLER.

T0 ALL THREE CHARGE MADE SAME TW0 BASIC P0INTS WITH THOUGHT THESE
TO ALL THREE CHARGE MADE SAME TWO BASIC POINTS WITH THOUGHT THESE
WOULD BE REL AYED T0 PRESIDENT PERE: (1) VENEZ2UELAN GOVERNMENT
WOULD BE REL AYED TO PRESIDENT PEREZ (1) VENEZUELAN GOVERNMENT
HAD MISREAD NEW Y0RK TIMES STORY: HAD ATTRIBUTED T0 DAVE
HAD MISREAD NEW YORK TIMES STORY, HAD ATTRIBUTED TO DAVID P

WHAT SEEMED TO BE AN EFFORT ON PHILLIPS' PART TO BE HELPFUL AND

(2) THE USG WAS STANDING PAT ON THE PRESIDENT'S LETTER, NO ONE ELSE
WAS SPEAKING, C0ULD OR W0ULD SPEAK FURTHER FOR THE USG ON THIS SU8JECT.
WAS SPEAKING, COULD OR WOULD SPEAK FURTHER FOR THE USG ON THIS SUBJECT.
AS FAR AS THE .S. WAS CONCERNED, ISSUE WAS CLOSED AND WE HOPED.
AS FAR AS THE US. WAS CONCERNED, ISSUE WAS CLOSED AND WE HOPED

GOV WOULD SEE IT THE SAME WAY
